title: After three years, Artemis 2 astronauts ready to launch
summary:

title: Live coverage: NASA to launch Artemis 2, its first Moon-bound mission with astronauts since 1972
summary: The four astronauts will spend more than nine days onboard their Orion spacecraft and is poised to pass the distance record set by Apollo 13 in 1970. Liftoff from Launch Complex 39B is scheduled for Wednesday, April 1, at 6:24 p.m. EDT (2224 UTC).

title: This is my third Orion launch, but it feels totally different
summary: The first two launches of Orion felt hollow, but NASA is finally on a better course.

title: Watch live: Artemis II launch
summary: The first launch opportunity for Artemis II, the first mission to bring astronauts towards the Moon in over 50 years, is set for 1 April at 18:24 local time (2 April at 00:24 CEST). Tune in from one hour before launch at 22:24 BST / 23:24 CEST on ESA Web TV to watch the launch.

title: Godspeed, Artemis II!
summary: NASA astronaut Jessica Meir took this photo of an Artemis program patch floating in the International Space Station’s cupola. title: How Artemis II Will Return Astronauts to the Moon
summary: NASA is preparing for the launch of Artemis II, the first mission to send humans to the moon since Apollo 17 in 1972. With launch now mere hours away, NASA and the astronauts are moving into final preparations for flight.
